Prompt
U
Oracle Events
OE 1. Pull the full list of overdue customer invoices using quickbooks_search_invoices (criteria to filter by Balance > 0 and DueDate < 2026-03-27, or an equivalent open-invoices query). Find six invoices more than 30 days past due as of April 26, 2026: INV-2026-001 (inv_greenleaf_01, Greenleaf Design Studio, $4,500, due 2026-02-15, 70 days overdue, billing contact rachel@greenleaf-design.com); INV-2026-002 (inv_stormcloud_01, StormCloud Analytics, $26,400, due 2026-02-16, 69 days overdue, billing contact tom.wexler@stormcloud.io); INV-2026-003 (inv_northwind_01, NorthWind Technologies, $60,000, due 2026-02-19, 66 days overdue, billing contact pam.kowalski@northwindtech.com); INV-2026-005 (inv_sunbelt_01, Sunbelt Venture Partners, $14,000, due 2026-02-20, 65 days overdue, billing contact carla.mendes@sunbeltvp.com); INV-2026-004 (inv_prairiedata_01, Prairie Data Consulting, $4,800, due 2026-02-21, 64 days overdue, billing contact grant@prairiedata.co); INV-2026-006 (inv_palmetto_01, Palmetto Community Foundation, $3,200, due 2026-02-21, 64 days overdue, billing contact jwhitfield@palmetto-foundation.org). Total overdue AR: $112,900.
OE 2. Pull the Vantage Distributed invoice using quickbooks_search_invoices (criteria on DocNumber "NMD-2026-0042" or CustomerRef cust-terraform-001). Find inv-terraform-001, DocNumber NMD-2026-0042, $462,500, TxnDate 2026-03-18, DueDate 2026-04-17 (9 days past due as of April 26, 2026), CustomerRef cust-terraform-001 / "Vantage Distributed". This is the largest single invoice MoveOps has ever issued and is now past due with no collections touchpoint sent.
OE 3. Search emails for NorthWind escalation context using search_emails (query: "NorthWind" or "Pam Kowalski" or "escalation"). Find email_northwind_escalation_david from Pam Kowalski in February flagging account issues, plus the April 24 formal escalation (email_email_7168baed8438) referencing PathFinder Logistics as a competitor being evaluated. Confirms NorthWind is in active churn crisis the "halfway out the door" account Marcus remembered. David Chen is running the retention conversation. A collections-toned dunning letter on the $60,000 INV-2026-003 could push Pam over.
OE 4. Cross-check Slack for the NorthWind churn save motion using conversations_search_messages (search_query: "NorthWind" or "PathFinder" or "Pam Kowalski"). Find recent messages confirming David Chen is leading the retention play and that the account is on active save-or-lose status. Any dunning outreach must be coordinated with David's retention track.
OE 5. Search emails for the Prairie Data account history using search_emails (query: "Prairie Data" or "Grant Halverson" or "inactive account manager"). Find Grant Halverson's February 26 escalation "Inactive Account Manager Prairie Data Consulting" (email_prairiedata_complaint_david) describing three unanswered emails and Catalina Dubois (catalina.dubois@moveops.com) going dark, plus Catalina's same-day apology thread. Confirms Prairie Data is the "AM went dark" bruised-relationship account Marcus remembered. Any standard dunning tone on the $4,800 INV-2026-004 will reopen the wound the account needs direct but calibrated outreach that acknowledges the February gap.
OE 6. Search emails and Airtable for the StormCloud operational incident using search_emails (query: "StormCloud" or "Jae-won" or "lease" or "Austin") and airtable_search_records (base_id: "appMoveOpsOps001", table_name: "Relocations", field_name: "Name", value: "Jae-won Kim"). Find the active Jae-won Kim Austin lease guarantor/countersignature incident with the temp-housing risk (Airtable record recJaeWonKimStormCloudAustin Company: StormCloud Analytics, Origin: Seattle WA, Destination: Austin TX, Status: In Progress, Special Requirements note "Lease still unsigned UrbanNest flagged landlord holdout on guarantor paperwork"). Confirms StormCloud has an unresolved operational issue sending a dunning letter on the $26,400 INV-2026-002 while MoveOps is still scrambling to fix Jae-won's lease would be tone-deaf. DO NOT DUN StormCloud.
OE 7. Cross-check Slack for the StormCloud incident thread using conversations_search_messages (search_query: "StormCloud" or "Jae-won" or "Austin lease"). Find messages in the operations/customer-engagement channel confirming the Austin lease guarantor/countersignature situation is still unresolved.
OE 8. Search Linear for the Sunbelt/Palmetto invoice correction ticket using linear_list_issues (query: "Sunbelt Palmetto" or "invoice attachment" or "reconstruct"). Find Linear issue #417 "Reconstruct Sunbelt/Palmetto invoice attachment exposure and issue corrected documentation" (not archived, due 2026-04-16, assigned to Marcus Thorne, priority 1). Note that #417 specifically covers a different invoice pair (INV-2026-0398 and INV-2026-0399 from the April attachment-swap incident), NOT the Q1 overdue invoices inv_sunbelt_01 ($14,000) and inv_palmetto_01 ($3,200). However, the existence of an active invoice-correction incident with both clients means MoveOps is in active remediation dunning their unrelated Q1 invoices while apologizing for the swap incident would be tone-deaf. DO NOT DUN Sunbelt or Palmetto while #417 is open.
OE 9. Search emails and Slack for any Greenleaf Design Studio context using search_emails (query: "Greenleaf" or "rachel@greenleaf-design.com") and conversations_search_messages (search_query: "Greenleaf"). Find no open incidents, no churn risk, no disputes. Greenleaf is the only overdue account safe to dun with a standard firm letter.
OE 10. Pull the AWS March bill using quickbooks_search_bills (criteria to match DocNumber "AWS-2026-03-INV"). Find bill-aws-march-001, DocNumber AWS-2026-03-INV, $42,870, VendorRef Amazon Web Services, DueDate 2026-04-15 (11 days past due as of April 26, 2026), posted to account acct-cloud-infra (Cloud Infrastructure). The $42,870 is roughly 3x the ~$14,300 normal baseline confirmed by Alejandro in #finance Slack.
OE 11. Search emails for Samira's AWS cost spike root cause analysis using search_emails (query: "Root Cause Analysis" and "AWS" or "Vantage" or "Samira Tariq"). Find Samira Tariq's email email-rootcause-001, timestamp 2026-03-30 23:35 UTC (2026-03-31 in forward timezones), subject "Root Cause Analysis: March AWS Cost Spike Vantage Distributed Onboarding", attributing the overage to the Vantage Distributed ~1,200-user onboarding wave starting 2026-03-21 (self-service portal batch imports driving EC2 autoscaling from 4 to 18 instances, plus S3 upload and RDS connection growth).
OE 12. Cross-check Slack for Samira's RCA summary and the pass-through decision status using conversations_search_messages (search_query: "pass-through" or "Vantage" or "cost spike", filter_in_channel: C009). Find Samira's April 1 RCA summary post in #root-cause-aws-spike (C009) with the highlights, plus the open thread asking whether the overage gets passed through to Vantage or absorbed by MoveOps. Confirm that NO pass-through decision has been logged yet the thread is still open, with Elena's March 31 email-rootcause-002 (timestamp 2026-03-31 00:38 UTC) instruction to Marcus to arrange a pricing model review meeting for next week (referenced by Marcus in his own C009 and C005 follow-up posts) being the most recent action. No pricing review has actually occurred yet. This is the blocker Marcus needs resolved before his conversation with Elena.
OE 13. Resolve the Vantage AP contact by searching CRM for the company using crm_search_companies (name: "Vantage Distributed") and then crm_search_contacts (full_name: "Rachel Nguyen"). Find company-terraform-digital (display name "Vantage Distributed") and contact-terraform-rachel-nguyen: Rachel Nguyen, VP of People Operations, rachel.nguyen@terraformdigital.com. Note the domain mismatch between the CRM customer name "Vantage Distributed" and Rachel's @terraformdigital.com email both refer to the same entity.
OE 14. Resolve Hana Kim's and David Chen's internal email addresses using contacts_search_contacts (query: "Hana Kim") and contacts_search_contacts (query: "David Chen"). Find hana.kim@moveops.com (Accountant, moveops_hana_kim) and david.chen@moveops.com (Customer Engagement Lead, moveops_david_chen). Both are internal MoveOps employees and live in contacts.contacts.json, NOT in crm.crm_contacts.json, so contacts_search_contacts is the correct tool (crm_search_contacts will return nothing for them).
OE 15. Resolve Pam Kowalski's CRM contact record using crm_search_contacts (full_name: "Pam Kowalski") so her contact_id can be attached to the NorthWind CRM engagement note. Pam is an external client contact and lives in crm.crm_contacts.json.
OE 16. Send a standard firm dunning email using send_email (sender: marcus.thorne@moveops.com, recipients: ["rachel@greenleaf-design.com"], subject referencing INV-2026-001 past due). Content references INV-2026-001, original due date 2026-02-15, 70 days past due, balance $4,500, politely requests payment or status update by the end of week, includes payment remittance instructions, and is signed Marcus Thorne, Head of Finance. Greenleaf is the only account with no open incident, no churn risk, and no dispute, so a standard dunning tone is appropriate.
OE 17. Send a calibrated non-dunning outreach email to Prairie Data using send_email (sender: marcus.thorne@moveops.com, recipients: ["grant@prairiedata.co"]). Content acknowledges the February account manager gap (Catalina going dark), apologizes for the silence, reframes INV-2026-004 ($4,800, 64 days past due) as a housekeeping item rather than a collections matter, offers a call, and avoids deadline or collections threat language. The bruised relationship requires an apology-first tone, not a dunning template.
OE 18. Send a carefully-calibrated outreach email to NorthWind using send_email (sender: marcus.thorne@moveops.com, recipients: ["pam.kowalski@northwindtech.com"], cc: ["david.chen@moveops.com"]). Content uses a soft relationship-first tone, explicitly separates this billing touchpoint from the retention conversation David Chen is running, makes no collections threats or deadlines, references INV-2026-003 ($60,000, 66 days past due), and offers to hold the invoice while Pam and David finish their conversation. NorthWind is in active churn crisis this note must not sound like a collections threat, and David is CC'd so the billing track stays coordinated with his retention work.
OE 19. Send the escalated past-due touchpoint to Rachel Nguyen at Vantage using send_email (sender: marcus.thorne@moveops.com, recipients: ["rachel.nguyen@terraformdigital.com"], subject referencing NMD-2026-0042 past due). Content references invoice NMD-2026-0042, $462,500, original due 2026-04-17, 9 days past due, requests immediate confirmation of payment status, asks Rachel to route internally to AP, offers to jump on a call, and uses a clearly escalated but still professional tone. This is the largest invoice MoveOps has ever issued and no one has reached out yet.
OE 20. Post the AR aging summary to #finance using conversations_add_message (channel_id: C005, payload containing the full aging picture). The summary lists the total $112,900 overdue AR across all six invoices with amounts; flags Greenleaf as dunned today; flags Prairie Data and NorthWind as soft-outreach only (with reasons bruised relationship and active churn crisis respectively); flags StormCloud, Sunbelt, and Palmetto as explicitly NOT dunned with reasons (StormCloud Jae-won Austin lease incident; Sunbelt and Palmetto Linear #417 invoice correction in progress); and notes the Vantage NMD-2026-0042 $462,500 escalated outreach. Gives Hana the full picture for her month-end close.
OE 21. Post the AWS bill deadline update to #root-cause-aws-spike using conversations_add_message (channel_id: C009, payload). The message notes that AWS-2026-03-INV for $42,870 was due 2026-04-15 and is now 11 days overdue (roughly 3x the ~$14,300 baseline per Alejandro), references Samira's RCA attributing the spike to Vantage onboarding, notes that no pass-through decision has been logged yet, and pushes explicitly for an owner and decision today because Marcus needs it before his conversation with Elena.
OE 22. Log the NorthWind billing-track engagement note using crm_create_engagement (engagement_type: "NOTE", company_ids: [NorthWind Technologies company id from crm_search_companies], contact_ids: [Pam Kowalski contact id from crm_search_contacts], title referencing INV-2026-003 billing track separation, body explaining that the billing track is being kept deliberately separate from the retention conversation David Chen is running, with no collections escalation until David gives the green light). Note: David Chen is an internal MoveOps employee in contacts.contacts.json, not a CRM contact, so he cannot be added to contact_ids instead his visibility is flagged via the note body and title.
OE 23. Schedule the aging walkthrough meeting using calendar_add_calendar_event (title referencing the Q1 AR aging walkthrough, start_datetime on the afternoon of 2026-04-27 (e.g., 2026-04-27T14:00:00), end_datetime approximately one hour later (e.g., 2026-04-27T15:00:00), attendees including marcus.thorne@moveops.com, hana.kim@moveops.com, and david.chen@moveops.com). "Tomorrow afternoon" from the universe date (Sunday April 26, 2026) resolves to Monday April 27, 2026 afternoon. The agenda covers the six overdue invoices, NorthWind coordination with the retention track, the Vantage NMD-2026-0042 escalation, the AWS pass-through decision status, and the month-end close impact for Hana.

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 26, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About MoveOps Inc.: MoveOps is a corporate relocation management company. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Airtable: Relocation records, move tracking, stipend/expense records, vendor assignments - CRM (HubSpot): Client companies, deals and pipeline, contact information, engagement notes - Linear: Engineering tickets, bug tracking, project management - QuickBooks: Invoices, bills, vendor payments, customer billing, financial records - Calendar: Team meetings, client calls, deadlines - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.
universe_idmcp-advanced-3-20-moveops-metadata

Oracle Events
OE 1. Pull the full list of overdue customer invoices using quickbooks_search_invoices (criteria to filter by Balance > 0 and DueDate < 2026-03-27, or an equivalent open-invoices query). Find six invoices more than 30 days past due as of April 26, 2026: INV-2026-001 (inv_greenleaf_01, Greenleaf Design Studio, $4,500, due 2026-02-15, 70 days overdue, billing contact rachel@greenleaf-design.com); INV-2026-002 (inv_stormcloud_01, StormCloud Analytics, $26,400, due 2026-02-16, 69 days overdue, billing contact tom.wexler@stormcloud.io); INV-2026-003 (inv_northwind_01, NorthWind Technologies, $60,000, due 2026-02-19, 66 days overdue, billing contact pam.kowalski@northwindtech.com); INV-2026-005 (inv_sunbelt_01, Sunbelt Venture Partners, $14,000, due 2026-02-20, 65 days overdue, billing contact carla.mendes@sunbeltvp.com); INV-2026-004 (inv_prairiedata_01, Prairie Data Consulting, $4,800, due 2026-02-21, 64 days overdue, billing contact grant@prairiedata.co); INV-2026-006 (inv_palmetto_01, Palmetto Community Foundation, $3,200, due 2026-02-21, 64 days overdue, billing contact jwhitfield@palmetto-foundation.org). Total overdue AR: $112,900.
OE 2. Pull the Vantage Distributed invoice using quickbooks_search_invoices (criteria on DocNumber "NMD-2026-0042" or CustomerRef cust-terraform-001). Find inv-terraform-001, DocNumber NMD-2026-0042, $462,500, TxnDate 2026-03-18, DueDate 2026-04-17 (9 days past due as of April 26, 2026), CustomerRef cust-terraform-001 / "Vantage Distributed". This is the largest single invoice MoveOps has ever issued and is now past due with no collections touchpoint sent.
OE 3. Search emails for NorthWind escalation context using search_emails (query: "NorthWind" or "Pam Kowalski" or "escalation"). OE 6. OE 11. Search emails for Samira's AWS cost spike root cause analysis using search_emails (query: "Root Cause Analysis" and "AWS" or "Vantage" or "Samira Tariq"). OE 12. Cross-check Slack for Samira's RCA summary and the pass-through decision status using conversations_search_messages (search_query: "pass-through" or "Vantage" or "cost spike", filter_in_channel: C009). Find Samira's April 1 RCA summary post in #root-cause-aws-spike (C009) with the highlights, plus the open thread asking whether the overage gets passed through to Vantage or absorbed by MoveOps. Confirm that NO pass-through decision has been logged yet the thread is still open, with Elena's March 31 email-rootcause-002 (timestamp 2026-03-31 00:38 UTC) instruction to Marcus to arrange a pricing model review meeting for next week (referenced by Marcus in his own C009 and C005 follow-up posts) being the most recent action. No pricing review has actually occurred yet. This is the blocker Marcus needs resolved before his conversation with Elena.
OE 13. Resolve the Vantage AP contact by searching CRM for the company using crm_search_companies (name: "Vantage Distributed") and then crm_search_contacts (full_name: "Rachel Nguyen"). Find company-terraform-digital (display name "Vantage Distributed") and contact-terraform-rachel-nguyen: Rachel Nguyen, VP of People Operations, rachel.nguyen@terraformdigital.com. Note the domain mismatch between the CRM customer name "Vantage Distributed" and Rachel's @terraformdigital.com email both refer to the same entity.
OE 14. Resolve Hana Kim's and David Chen's internal email addresses using contacts_search_contacts (query: "Hana Kim") and contacts_search_contacts (query: "David Chen"). Find hana.kim@moveops.com (Accountant, moveops_hana_kim) and david.chen@moveops.com (Customer Engagement Lead, moveops_david_chen). Both are internal MoveOps employees and live in contacts.contacts.json, NOT in crm.crm_contacts.json, so contacts_search_contacts is the correct tool (crm_search_contacts will return nothing for them).
OE 15. Resolve Pam Kowalski's CRM contact record using crm_search_contacts (full_name: "Pam Kowalski") so her contact_id can be attached to the NorthWind CRM engagement note. Pam is an external client contact and lives in crm.crm_contacts.json.
OE 16. Send a standard firm dunning email using send_email (sender: marcus.thorne@moveops.com, recipients: ["rachel@greenleaf-design.com"], subject referencing INV-2026-001 past due). Content references INV-2026-001, original due date 2026-02-15, 70 days past due, balance $4,500, politely requests payment or status update by the end of week, includes payment remittance instructions, and is signed Marcus Thorne, Head of Finance. Greenleaf is the only account with no open incident, no churn risk, and no dispute, so a standard dunning tone is appropriate.
OE 17. Send a calibrated non-dunning outreach email to Prairie Data using send_email (sender: marcus.thorne@moveops.com, recipients: ["grant@prairiedata.co"]). Content acknowledges the February account manager gap (Catalina going dark), apologizes for the silence, reframes INV-2026-004 ($4,800, 64 days past due) as a housekeeping item rather than a collections matter, offers a call, and avoids deadline or collections threat language. The bruised relationship requires an apology-first tone, not a dunning template.
OE 18. Send a carefully-calibrated outreach email to NorthWind using send_email (sender: marcus.thorne@moveops.com, recipients: ["pam.kowalski@northwindtech.com"], cc: ["david.chen@moveops.com"]). Content uses a soft relationship-first tone, explicitly separates this billing touchpoint from the retention conversation David Chen is running, makes no collections threats or deadlines, references INV-2026-003 ($60,000, 66 days past due), and offers to hold the invoice while Pam and David finish their conversation. NorthWind is in active churn crisis this note must not sound like a collections threat, and David is CC'd so the billing track stays coordinated with his retention work.
OE 19. Send the escalated past-due touchpoint to Rachel Nguyen at Vantage using send_email (sender: marcus.thorne@moveops.com, recipients: ["rachel.nguyen@terraformdigital.com"], subject referencing NMD-2026-0042 past due). Content references invoice NMD-2026-0042, $462,500, original due 2026-04-17, 9 days past due, requests immediate confirmation of payment status, asks Rachel to route internally to AP, offers to jump on a call, and uses a clearly escalated but still professional tone. This is the largest invoice MoveOps has ever issued and no one has reached out yet.
OE 20. Post the AR aging summary to #finance using conversations_add_message (channel_id: C005, payload containing the full aging picture). The summary lists the total $112,900 overdue AR across all six invoices with amounts; flags Greenleaf as dunned today; flags Prairie Data and NorthWind as soft-outreach only (with reasons bruised relationship and active churn crisis respectively); flags StormCloud, Sunbelt, and Palmetto as explicitly NOT dunned with reasons (StormCloud Jae-won Austin lease incident; Sunbelt and Palmetto Linear #417 invoice correction in progress); and notes the Vantage NMD-2026-0042 $462,500 escalated outreach. Gives Hana the full picture for her month-end close.
OE 21. Post the AWS bill deadline update to #root-cause-aws-spike using conversations_add_message (channel_id: C009, payload). The message notes that AWS-2026-03-INV for $42,870 was due 2026-04-15 and is now 11 days overdue (roughly 3x the ~$14,300 baseline per Alejandro), references Samira's RCA attributing the spike to Vantage onboarding, notes that no pass-through decision has been logged yet, and pushes explicitly for an owner and decision today because Marcus needs it before his conversation with Elena.
OE 22. Log the NorthWind billing-track engagement note using crm_create_engagement (engagement_type: "NOTE", company_ids: [NorthWind Technologies company id from crm_search_companies], contact_ids: [Pam Kowalski contact id from crm_search_contacts], title referencing INV-2026-003 billing track separation, body explaining that the billing track is being kept deliberately separate from the retention conversation David Chen is running, with no collections escalation until David gives the green light). Note: David Chen is an internal MoveOps employee in contacts.contacts.json, not a CRM contact, so he cannot be added to contact_ids instead his visibility is flagged via the note body and title.
OE 23. Schedule the aging walkthrough meeting using calendar_add_calendar_event (title referencing the Q1 AR aging walkthrough, start_datetime on the afternoon of 2026-04-27 (e.g., 2026-04-27T14:00:00), end_datetime approximately one hour later (e.g., 2026-04-27T15:00:00), attendees including marcus.thorne@moveops.com, hana.kim@moveops.com, and david.chen@moveops.com). "Tomorrow afternoon" from the universe date (Sunday April 26, 2026) resolves to Monday April 27, 2026 afternoon. The agenda covers the six overdue invoices, NorthWind coordination with the retention track, the Vantage NMD-2026-0042 escalation, the AWS pass-through decision status, and the month-end close impact for Hana.

✓Run #6The #root-cause-aws-spike Slack message explicitly states 'The pass-through vs. absorb decision has NOT been made' and lists three options: pass through to Vantage, absorb internally, or hybrid. The message actively pushes for a decision.

36 The final response identifies Samira Tariq's root cause analysis as attributing the March 2026 AWS spike to Vantage Distributed's onboarding wave (\~1,200 users) and cites the primary driver (self-service portal batch imports or EC2 autoscaling from 4 to 18 instances) plus at least one secondary mechanism (S3 upload growth or RDS connection growth).
outcome
 ✓✓✓✓✗✓ 83%
✓Run #1The final response attributes the spike to 'Vantage onboarding of 1,200+ users starting March 21 drove autoscaling from 4→18 EC2 instances' (primary driver) plus '47K document uploads' (S3 growth) and 'DB connection spikes' (RDS). Primary driver and at least one secondary mechanism are present. PASS.
✓Run #2The final response attributes the spike to 'Vantage Distributed onboarding of 1,200+ users starting March 21 drove autoscaling from 4→18 EC2 instances, 47K document uploads, and DB connection spikes' — citing the primary driver (EC2 autoscaling 4→18) plus secondary mechanisms (document uploads / DB connections).
✓Run #3The final response attributes the root cause to 'Vantage Distributed onboarding of 1,200+ users', with primary driver 'autoscaling from 4→18 EC2 instances', and secondary mechanisms '47K document uploads' (S3) and '340 DB connections (up from 50)' (RDS). All required elements are present.
✓Run #4The final response attributes the root cause to 'Vantage Distributed onboarding 1,200+ users starting March 21. Self-service portal drove autoscaling 4→18 EC2 instances' (primary driver) and mentions '47K document uploads' and 'DB connections from 50→340' as secondary mechanisms.
✗Run #5The final response only states Samira's root cause as 'Vantage Distributed's 1,200-user onboarding starting March 21 caused legitimate 3x autoscaling' without naming the primary mechanism (EC2 autoscaling 4→18 instances or self-service portal batch imports) or any secondary mechanism (S3 or RDS). The detailed RCA specifics appear only in the Slack post, not the final response.

All required elements are present.
